tEn: LIMA: Less Is More for Alignment
tZh: LIMA：少即是多——对齐的简约之道
sEn: Almost all knowledge in large language models is learned during pretraining, and only limited instruction tuning data is necessary to teach models to produce high quality output.
sZh: 大型语言模型中的几乎所有知识都是在预训练阶段学到的，只需有限的指令微调数据即可教会模型生成高质量输出。
absEn: Large language models are trained in two stages: (1) unsupervised pretraining from raw text, to learn general-purpose representations, and (2) large scale instruction tuning and reinforcement learning, to better align to end tasks and user preferences. We measure the relative importance of these two stages by training LIMA, a 65B parameter LLaMa language model fine-tuned with the standard supervised loss on only 1,000 carefully curated prompts and responses, without any reinforcement learning or human preference modeling. LIMA demonstrates remarkably strong performance, learning to follow specific response formats from only a handful of examples in the training data, including complex queries that range from planning trip itineraries to speculating about alternate history. Moreover, the model tends to generalize well to unseen tasks that did not appear in the training data. In a controlled human study, responses from LIMA are either equivalent or strictly preferred to GPT-4 in 43% of cases; this statistic is as high as 58% when compared to Bard and 65% versus DaVinci003, which was trained with human feedback. Taken together, these results strongly suggest that almost all knowledge in large language models is learned during pretraining, and only limited instruction tuning data is necessary to teach models to produce high quality output.
absZh: 大型语言模型的训练分为两个阶段：（1）从原始文本进行无监督预训练，学习通用表示；（2）大规模指令微调和强化学习，以更好地对齐最终任务和用户偏好。我们通过训练LIMA来衡量这两个阶段的相对重要性。LIMA是一个65B参数的LLaMa语言模型，仅使用1000个精心挑选的提示和响应进行标准监督损失微调，未使用任何强化学习或人类偏好建模。LIMA表现出色，仅从训练数据中的少量示例就学会了遵循特定的响应格式，包括从规划旅行行程到推测替代历史等复杂查询。此外，该模型对训练数据中未出现的未见任务也具有良好的泛化能力。在受控人类研究中，LIMA的响应在43%的情况下与GPT-4相当或更受青睐；与Bard相比这一比例为58%，与经过人类反馈训练的DaVinci003相比则为65%。这些结果强烈表明，大型语言模型中的几乎所有知识都是在预训练阶段学到的，只需有限的指令微调数据即可教会模型生成高质量输出。

contrib.en: Shows that fine-tuning a 65B LLaMa on only 1,000 curated examples achieves strong alignment.
contrib.zh: 表明仅用 1000 个精选示例微调 65B LLaMa 即可实现强对齐。

contrib.en: Proposes the Superficial Alignment Hypothesis: knowledge is from pretraining, alignment learns style.
contrib.zh: 提出浅层对齐假说：知识来自预训练，对齐学习风格。

contrib.en: LIMA matches or beats GPT-4 in 43% of cases, and outperforms RLHF-trained DaVinci003.
contrib.zh: LIMA 在 43% 的案例中匹配或优于 GPT-4，并超越经 RLHF 训练的 DaVinci003。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that scaling data quantity without diversity yields diminishing returns.
contrib.zh: 表明不增加多样性的数据规模扩张收益递减。

contrib.en: Shows multi-turn dialogue ability with only 30 additional dialogue chains.
contrib.zh: 表明仅添加 30 条对话链即可具备多轮对话能力。

contrib.en: Provides absolute evaluation: 88% of LIMA responses meet prompt requirements, 50% are excellent.
contrib.zh: 提供绝对评估：LIMA 88% 的响应满足提示要求，50% 为优秀。

limits.en: Manual curation of 1,000 examples is labor-intensive and hard to scale.
limits.zh: 手动策划 1000 个示例劳动密集且难以规模化。

limits.en: LIMA is less robust than production models; adversarial prompts can cause weak responses.
limits.zh: LIMA 不如生产级模型鲁棒；对抗性提示可能导致弱响应。

limits.en: Limited to single-turn fine-tuning; multi-turn dialogue requires additional data.
limits.zh: 限于单轮微调；多轮对话需要额外数据。

limits.en: Safety performance is limited; implicit malicious intents may bypass safeguards.
limits.zh: 安全性能有限；隐含恶意意图可能绕过防护。

limits.en: Results may not generalize to other model sizes or architectures without further study.
limits.zh: 结果可能无法泛化到其他模型规模或架构，需进一步研究。

items.en: Large language models are trained in two stages: (1) unsupervised pretraining from raw text, to learn general-purpose representations, and (2) large scale instruction tuning and reinforcement learning, to better align to end tasks and user preferences. We measure the relative importance of these two stages by training LIMA, a 65B parameter LLaMa language model fine-tuned with the standard supervised loss on only 1,000 carefully curated prompts and responses, without any reinforcement learning or human preference modeling. LIMA demonstrates remarkably strong performance, learning to follow specific response formats from only a handful of examples in the training data, including complex queries that range from planning trip itineraries to speculating about alternate history. Moreover, the model tends to generalize well to unseen tasks that did not appear in the training data. In a controlled human study, responses from LIMA are either equivalent or strictly preferred to GPT-4 in 43% of cases; this statistic is as high as 58% when compared to Bard and 65% versus DaVinci003, which was trained with human feedback. Taken together, these results strongly suggest that almost all knowledge in large language models is learned during pretraining, and only limited instruction tuning data is necessary to teach models to produce high quality output.
items.zh: 大型语言模型的训练分为两个阶段：（1）从原始文本进行无监督预训练，以学习通用表示；（2）大规模指令微调和强化学习，以更好地对齐最终任务和用户偏好。我们通过训练LIMA来衡量这两个阶段的相对重要性，LIMA是一个65B参数的LLaMa语言模型，仅使用1,000个精心策划的提示和响应，在标准监督损失下进行微调，无需任何强化学习或人类偏好建模。LIMA表现出非常强大的性能，仅从训练数据中的少量示例中学习遵循特定的响应格式，包括从规划旅行行程到推测替代历史等复杂查询。此外，该模型倾向于很好地泛化到训练数据中未出现的未见任务。在受控的人类研究中，LIMA的响应在43%的情况下与GPT-4相当或更受青睐；与Bard相比，这一统计数字高达58%，与经过人类反馈训练的DaVinci003相比则为65%。综合来看，这些结果强烈表明，大型语言模型中的几乎所有知识都是在预训练期间学习的，只需要有限的指令微调数据即可教会模型生成高质量输出。

items.en: Language models are pretrained to predict the next token at an incredible scale, allowing them to learn general-purpose representations that can be transferred to nearly any language understanding or generation task. To enable this transfer, various methods for aligning language models have thus been proposed, primarily focusing on instruction tuning (Mishra et al., 2021; Wei et al., 2022a; Sanh et al., 2022) over large multi-million-example datasets (Chung et al., 2022; Beeching et al., 2023; Köpf et al., 2023), and more recently reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) (Bai et al., 2022a; Ouyang et al., 2022), collected over millions of interactions with human annotators. Existing alignment methods require significant amounts of compute and specialized data to achieve ChatGPT-level performance. However, we demonstrate that, given a strong pretrained language model, remarkably strong performance can be achieved by simply fine-tuning on 1,000 carefully curated training examples.
items.zh: 语言模型以惊人的规模进行预训练以预测下一个词，使它们能够学习可迁移到几乎任何语言理解或生成任务的通用表示。为了实现这种迁移，人们提出了各种对齐语言模型的方法，主要侧重于在大型百万示例数据集上的指令微调（Mishra等人，2021；Wei等人，2022a；Sanh等人，2022），以及最近基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）（Bai等人，2022a；Ouyang等人，2022），这些方法收集了数百万次与人类标注者的交互。现有的对齐方法需要大量的算力和专门数据才能达到ChatGPT级别的性能。然而，我们证明，给定一个强大的预训练语言模型，仅通过对1,000个精心策划的训练示例进行微调，即可实现非常强大的性能。

items.en: We hypothesize that alignment can be a simple process where the model learns the style or format for interacting with users, to expose the knowledge and capabilities that were already acquired during pretraining. To test this hypothesis, we curate 1,000 examples that approximate real user prompts and high-quality responses. We select 750 top questions and answers from community forums, such as Stack Exchange and wikiHow, sampling for quality and diversity. In addition, we manually write 250 examples of prompts and responses, while optimizing for task diversity and emphasizing a uniform response style in the spirit of an AI assistant. Finally, we train LIMA, a pretrained 65B-parameter LLaMa model (Touvron et al., 2023) fine-tuned on this set of 1,000 demonstrations.
items.zh: 我们假设对齐可以是一个简单的过程，其中模型学习与用户交互的风格或格式，以暴露预训练期间已经获得的知识和能力。为了验证这一假设，我们策划了1,000个近似真实用户提示和高质量响应的示例。我们从社区论坛（如Stack Exchange和wikiHow）中挑选了750个高质量问题和答案，并进行了质量和多样性采样。此外，我们手动编写了250个提示和响应示例，同时优化任务多样性并强调以AI助手精神统一的响应风格。最后，我们训练了LIMA，这是一个预训练的65B参数LLaMa模型（Touvron等人，2023），在这组1,000个演示上进行了微调。

items.en: We compare LIMA to state-of-the-art language models and products across 300 challenging test prompts. In a human preference study, we find that LIMA outperforms RLHF-trained DaVinci003 from OpenAI, which was trained with RLHF, as well as a 65B-parameter reproduction of Alpaca (Taori et al., 2023), which was trained on 52,000 examples. While humans typically prefer responses from GPT-4, Claude, and Bard over LIMA, this is not always the case; LIMA produces equal or preferrable responses in 43%, 46%, and 58% of the cases, respectively. Repeating the human preference annotations with GPT-4 as the annotator corroborates our findings. Analyzing LIMA responses on an absolute scale reveals that 88% meet the prompt requirements, and 50% are considered excellent.
items.zh: 我们将LIMA与最先进的语言模型和产品在300个具有挑战性的测试提示上进行了比较。在人类偏好研究中，我们发现LIMA优于OpenAI的经过RLHF训练的DaVinci003（该模型使用RLHF训练）以及一个在52,000个示例上训练的65B参数Alpaca复现版本（Taori等人，2023）。虽然人类通常更喜欢GPT-4、Claude和Bard的响应而非LIMA，但情况并非总是如此；LIMA在43%、46%和58%的情况下分别产生相当或更优的响应。使用GPT-4作为标注者重复人类偏好注释证实了我们的发现。对LIMA响应的绝对尺度分析显示，88%满足提示要求，50%被认为优秀。

items.en: Ablation experiments reveal vastly diminishing returns when scaling up data quantity without also scaling up prompt diversity, alongside major gains when optimizing data quality. In addition, despite having zero dialogue examples, we find that LIMA can conduct coherent multi-turn dialogue, and that this ability can be dramatically improved by adding only 30 hand-crafted dialogue chains to the training set. Overall, these remarkable findings demonstrate the power of pretraining and its relative importance over large-scale instruction tuning and reinforcement learning approaches.
items.zh: 消融实验表明，在不增加提示多样性的情况下扩大数据量会带来急剧递减的回报，而优化数据质量则带来重大收益。此外，尽管没有对话示例，我们发现LIMA可以进行连贯的多轮对话，并且通过仅向训练集添加30个手工制作的对话链，这种能力可以得到显著提高。总体而言，这些显著发现证明了预训练的力量及其相对于大规模指令微调和强化学习方法的重要性。

items.en: We define the Superficial Alignment Hypothesis: A model’s knowledge and capabilities are learnt almost entirely during pretraining, while alignment teaches it which subdistribution of formats should be used when interacting with users. If this hypothesis is correct, and alignment is largely about learning style, then a corollary of the Superficial Alignment Hypothesis is that one could sufficiently tune a pretrained language model with a rather small set of examples (Kirstain et al., 2021).
items.zh: 我们定义了表面对齐假设：模型的知识和能力几乎完全在预训练期间学习，而对齐则教会它在与用户交互时应使用哪种格式的子分布。如果这个假设是正确的，并且对齐主要是关于学习风格，那么表面对齐假设的一个推论是，可以用相当小的示例集来充分微调预训练语言模型（Kirstain等人，2021）。

items.en: To that end, we collect a dataset of 1,000 prompts and responses, where the outputs (responses) are stylistically aligned with each other, but the inputs (prompts) are diverse. Specifically, we seek outputs in the style of a helpful AI assistant. We curate such examples from a variety of sources, primarily split into community Q&A forums and manually authored examples. We also collect a test set of 300 prompts and a development set of 50. Table 1 shows an overview of the different data sources and provides some statistics (see Appendix A for a selection of training examples).
items.zh: 为此，我们收集了一个包含1,000个提示和响应的数据集，其中输出（响应）在风格上相互对齐，但输入（提示）是多样化的。具体来说，我们寻求以有帮助的AI助手风格输出。我们从多种来源策划此类示例，主要分为社区问答论坛和手动编写的示例。我们还收集了300个提示的测试集和50个提示的开发集。表1显示了不同数据源的概述并提供了一些统计数据（参见附录A中的训练示例选择）。

items.en: We collect data from three community Q&A websites: Stack Exchange, wikiHow, and the Pushshift Reddit Dataset (Baumgartner et al., 2020). Largely speaking, answers from Stack Exchange and wikiHow are well-aligned with the behavior of a helpful AI agent, and can therefore be mined automatically, whereas highly upvoted Reddit answers tend to be humorous or trolling, requiring a more manual approach to curate responses that follow the appropriate style.
items.zh: 我们从三个社区问答网站收集数据：Stack Exchange、wikiHow和Pushshift Reddit数据集（Baumgartner等人，2020）。总的来说，Stack Exchange和wikiHow的答案与有帮助的AI智能体的行为高度一致，因此可以自动挖掘，而Reddit上高赞的答案往往幽默或恶搞，需要更手动的方法来策划符合适当风格的响应。

items.en: Stack Exchange contains 179 online communities (exchanges), each one dedicated to a specific topic, with the most popular one being programming (Stack Overflow). Users can post questions, answers, comments and upvote (or downvote) all of the above. Thanks to active community members and moderators, Stack Exchange has successfully maintained a high bar for content quality.
items.zh: Stack Exchange包含179个在线社区（交流区），每个社区专注于特定主题，其中最受欢迎的是编程（Stack Overflow）。用户可以发布问题、答案、评论并点赞（或点踩）所有内容。得益于活跃的社区成员和版主，Stack Exchange成功保持了高质量的内容标准。

items.en: We apply both quality and diversity controls when sampling from Stack Exchange. First, we divide the exchanges into 75 STEM exchanges (including programming, math, physics, etc.) and 99 other (English, cooking, travel, and more); we discard 5 niche exchanges. We then sample 200 questions and answers from each set using a temperature of τ=3𝜏3\tau=3 to get a more uniform sample of the different domains. Within each exchange, we take the questions with the highest score that are self-contained in the title (no body). We then select the top answer for each question, assuming it had a strong positive score (at least 10). To conform with the style of a helpful AI assistant, we automatically filter answers that are too short (less than 1200 characters), too long (more than 4096 characters), written in the first person (“I”, “my”), or reference other answers (“as mentioned”, “stack exchange”, etc); we also remove links, images, and other HTML tags from the response, retaining only code blocks and lists. Since Stack Exchange questions contain both a title and a description, we randomly select the title as the prompt for some examples, and the description for others.
items.zh: 在从Stack Exchange采样时，我们应用了质量和多样性控制。首先，我们将交流区分为75个STEM交流区（包括编程、数学、物理等）和99个其他交流区（英语、烹饪、旅行等）；我们丢弃了5个小众交流区。然后，我们从每组中采样200个问题和答案，使用温度τ=3以获得不同领域的更均匀样本。在每个交流区内，我们选取标题中自包含（无正文）且得分最高的问题。然后，我们为每个问题选择最佳答案，假设其具有强正得分（至少10）。为了符合有帮助的AI助手的风格，我们自动过滤过短（少于1200个字符）、过长（超过4096个字符）、以第一人称（“我”、“我的”）书写或引用其他答案（“如前所述”、“stack exchange”等）的答案；我们还从响应中删除链接、图像和其他HTML标签，仅保留代码块和列表。由于Stack Exchange问题包含标题和描述，我们随机选择标题作为某些示例的提示，选择描述作为其他示例的提示。

items.en: wikiHow is an online wiki-style publication featuring over 240,000 how-to articles on a variety of topics. Anyone can contribute to wikiHow, though articles are heavily moderated, resulting in almost universally high-quality content. We sample 200 articles from wikiHow, sampling a category first (out of 19) and then an article within it to ensure diversity. We use the title as the prompt (e.g. “How to cook an omelette?”) and the article’s body as the response. We replace the typical “This article...” beginning with “The following answer...”, and apply a number of preprocessing heuristics to prune links, images, and certain sections of the text.
items.zh: wikiHow是一个在线维基风格出版物，包含超过240,000篇关于各种主题的操作指南文章。任何人都可以为wikiHow贡献内容，但文章经过严格审核，导致内容几乎普遍高质量。我们从wikiHow中采样200篇文章，首先采样一个类别（共19个），然后从中选择一篇文章以确保多样性。我们使用标题作为提示（例如“如何煎蛋饼？”），文章正文作为响应。我们将典型的“本文...”开头替换为“以下答案...”，并应用一系列预处理启发式规则来修剪链接、图像和文本的某些部分。

items.en: Reddit is one of the most popular websites in the world, allowing users to share, discuss, and upvote content in user-created subreddits. Due to its immense popularity, Reddit is geared more towards entertaining fellow users rather than helping; it is quite often the case that witty, sarcastic comments will obtain more votes than serious, informative comments to a post. We thus restrict our sample to two subsets, r/AskReddit and r/WritingPrompts, and manually select examples from within the most upvoted posts in each community. From r/AskReddit we find 70 self-contained prompts (title only, no body), which we use for the test set, since the top answers are not necessarily reliable. The WritingPrompts subreddit contains premises of fictional stories, which other users are then encouraged to creatively complete. We find 150 prompts and high-quality responses, encompassing topics such as love poems and short science fiction stories, which we add to the training set. All data instances were mined from the Pushshift Reddit Dataset (Baumgartner et al., 2020).
items.zh: Reddit是世界上最受欢迎的网站之一，允许用户在用户创建的子版块中分享、讨论和点赞内容。由于其巨大的受欢迎程度，Reddit更倾向于娱乐其他用户而不是帮助；通常情况下，诙谐、讽刺的评论比严肃、信息丰富的评论获得更多点赞。因此，我们将样本限制在两个子集：r/AskReddit和r/WritingPrompts，并手动从每个社区中点赞最多的帖子中选择示例。从r/AskReddit中，我们找到70个自包含的提示（仅标题，无正文），用于测试集，因为最佳答案不一定可靠。WritingPrompts子版块包含虚构故事的前提，鼓励其他用户创造性地完成。我们找到150个提示和高质量响应，涵盖爱情诗和短篇科幻故事等主题，并将其添加到训练集中。所有数据实例均来自Pushshift Reddit数据集（Baumgartner等人，2020）。

items.en: To further diversify our data beyond questions asked by users in online communities, we collect prompts from ourselves (the authors of this work). We designate two sets of authors, Group A and Group B, to create 250 prompts each, inspired by their own interests or those of their friends.111Despite our efforts to prevent leakage, there was significant contact between the groups before the annotation process, which resulted in certain shared priors that can be observed in the data. We select 200 prompts from Group A for training and 50 prompts as a held-out development set. After filtering some problematic prompts, the remaining 230 prompts from Group B are used for test.
items.zh: 为了进一步使数据多样化，超越用户在在线社区中提出的问题，我们从自身（本文作者）收集提示。我们指定两组作者，A组和B组，每组创建250个提示，灵感来自他们自己或朋友的兴趣。尽管我们努力防止泄露，但在标注过程之前两组之间存在显著接触，导致数据中可观察到某些共享先验。我们从A组中选择200个提示用于训练，50个提示作为保留的开发集。在过滤一些有问题的提示后，B组剩余的230个提示用于测试。

items.en: We supplement the 200 training prompts with high-quality answers, which we write ourselves. While authoring answers, we try to set a uniform tone that is appropriate for a helpful AI assistant. Specifically, many prompts will be answered with some acknowledgment of the question followed by the answer itself. Preliminary experiments show that this consistent format generally improves model performance; we hypothesize that it assists the model in forming a chain of thought, similar to the “let’s think step-by-step” prompt (Kojima et al., 2022; Wei et al., 2022b).
items.zh: 我们为200个训练提示补充了高质量答案，这些答案由我们自己编写。在编写答案时，我们试图设定适合有帮助的AI助手的统一语气。具体来说，许多提示将以对问题的某种确认后跟答案本身来回答。初步实验表明，这种一致的格式通常能提高模型性能；我们假设它有助于模型形成思维链，类似于“让我们逐步思考”的提示（Kojima等人，2022；Wei等人，2022b）。

items.en: We also include 13 training prompts with some degree of toxicity or malevolence. We carefully write responses that partially or fully reject the command, and explain why the assistant will not comply. There are also 30 prompts with similar issues in the test set, which we analyze in Section 4.3.
items.zh: 我们还包含了13个具有一定毒性或恶意的训练提示。我们仔细编写了部分或完全拒绝命令的响应，并解释助手为何不遵守。测试集中也有30个类似问题的提示，我们在第4.3节中进行了分析。

items.en: In addition to our manually authored examples, we sample 50 training examples from Super-Natural Instructions (Wang et al., 2022b). Specifically, we select 50 natural language generation tasks such as summarization, paraphrasing, and style transfer, and pick a single random example from each one. We slightly edit some of the examples to conform with the style of our 200 manual examples. While the distribution of potential user prompts is arguably different from the distribution of tasks in Super-Natural Instructions, our intuition is that this small sample adds diversity to the overall mix of training examples, and can potentially increase model robustness.
items.zh: 除了我们手动编写的示例外，我们还从Super-Natural Instructions（Wang等人，2022b）中采样了50个训练示例。具体来说，我们选择了50个自然语言生成任务，如摘要、释义和风格迁移，并从每个任务中挑选一个随机示例。我们稍微编辑了一些示例，使其符合我们200个手动示例的风格。虽然潜在用户提示的分布可能与Super-Natural Instructions中的任务分布不同，但我们的直觉是，这个小样本增加了整体训练示例的多样性，并可能提高模型鲁棒性。

items.en: Manually creating diverse prompts and authoring rich responses in a uniform style is laborious. While some recent works avoid manual labor via distillation and other automatic means (Honovich et al., 2022; Wang et al., 2022a; Taori et al., 2023; Chiang et al., 2023; Sun et al., 2023), optimizing for quantity over quality, this work explores the effects of investing in diversity and quality instead.
items.zh: 手动创建多样化的提示并以统一风格编写丰富的响应是费力的。虽然最近的一些工作通过蒸馏和其他自动手段避免了手动劳动（Honovich等人，2022；Wang等人，2022a；Taori等人，2023；Chiang等人，2023；Sun等人，2023），优化数量而非质量，但本文探讨了投资于多样性和质量的效果。

items.en: We train LIMA (Less Is More for Alignment) using the following protocol. Starting from LLaMa 65B (Touvron et al., 2023), we fine-tune on our 1,000-example alignment training set. To differentiate between each speaker (user and assistant), we introduce a special end-of-turn token (EOT) at the end of each utterance; this token plays the same role as EOS of halting generation, but avoids conflation with any other meaning that the pretrained model may have imbued into the preexisting EOS token.
items.zh: 我们使用以下协议训练LIMA（Less Is More for Alignment）。从LLaMa 65B（Touvron等人，2023）开始，我们在1,000示例的对齐训练集上进行微调。为了区分每个说话者（用户和助手），我们在每个话语的末尾引入一个特殊的轮次结束标记（EOT）；该标记与EOS停止生成的作用相同，但避免了与预训练模型可能赋予现有EOS标记的任何其他含义混淆。

items.en: We follow standard fine-tuning hyperparameters: we fine-tune for 15 epochs using AdamW (Loshchilov and Hutter, 2017) with β1=0.9,β2=0.95formulae-sequencesubscript𝛽10.9subscript𝛽20.95\beta_{1}=0.9,\beta_{2}=0.95, and weight decay of 0.10.10.1. Without warmup steps, we set the initial learning rate to 1​e−51𝑒51e-5 and linearly decaying to 1​e−61𝑒61e-6 by the end of training. The batch size is set to 32 examples (64 for smaller models), and texts longer than 2048 tokens are trimmed. One notable deviation from the norm is the use of residual dropout; we follow Ouyang et al. (2022) and apply dropout over residual connections, starting at pd=0.0subscript𝑝𝑑0.0p_{d}=0.0 at the bottom layer and linearly raising the rate to pd=0.3subscript𝑝𝑑0.3p_{d}=0.3 at the last layer (pd=0.2subscript𝑝𝑑0.2p_{d}=0.2 for smaller models). We find that perplexity does not correlate with generation quality, and thus manually select checkpoints between the 5th and the 10th epochs using the held-out 50-example development set.222See Appendix B for a more detailed study comparing validation perplexity and generation quality.
items.zh: 我们遵循标准的微调超参数：使用AdamW（Loshchilov和Hutter，2017）微调15个epoch，β1=0.9，β2=0.95，权重衰减为0.1。没有预热步骤，我们将初始学习率设置为1e-5，并在训练结束时线性衰减到1e-6。批量大小设置为32个示例（较小模型为64个），文本长度超过2048个标记的被截断。一个值得注意的偏差是使用残差丢弃；我们遵循Ouyang等人（2022），在残差连接上应用丢弃，从底层pd=0.0开始，线性增加到最后一层pd=0.3（较小模型pd=0.2）。我们发现困惑度与生成质量不相关，因此使用保留的50示例开发集手动选择第5到第10个epoch之间的检查点。

items.en: We evaluate LIMA by comparing it to state-of-the-art language models, and find that it outperforms OpenAI’s RLHF-based DaVinci003 and a 65B-parameter reproduction of Alpaca trained on 52,000 examples, and often produces better-or-equal responses than GPT-4. Analyzing of LIMA generations finds that 50% of its outputs are considered excellent. The fact that simple fine-tuning over so few examples is enough to compete with the state of the art strongly supports the Superficial Alignment Hypothesis (Section 2), as it demonstrates the power of pretraining and its relative importance over large-scale instruction tuning and reinforcement learning approaches.
items.zh: 我们通过将LIMA与最先进的语言模型进行比较来评估它，发现它优于OpenAI基于RLHF的DaVinci003和在52,000个示例上训练的65B参数Alpaca复现版本，并且经常产生比GPT-4更好或相当的响应。对LIMA生成结果的分析发现，50%的输出被认为是优秀的。在如此少的示例上进行简单微调就足以与最先进技术竞争，这一事实强烈支持了表面对齐假设（第2节），因为它证明了预训练的力量及其相对于大规模指令微调和强化学习方法的重要性。

items.en: To compare LIMA to other models, we generate a single response for each test prompt. We then ask crowd workers to compare LIMA outputs to each of the baselines and label which one they prefer. We repeat this experiment, replacing human crowd workers with GPT-4, finding similar agreement levels.
items.zh: 为了将LIMA与其他模型进行比较，我们为每个测试提示生成一个响应。然后，我们要求众包工作者将LIMA的输出与每个基线进行比较，并标记他们更喜欢哪一个。我们重复这个实验，用GPT-4代替人类众包工作者，发现相似的一致性水平。

items.en: We compare LIMA to five baselines: Alpaca 65B (Taori et al., 2023) – we finetune LLaMa 65B (Touvron et al., 2023) on the 52,000 examples in the Alpaca training set (Taori et al., 2023); OpenAI’s DaVinci003,333https://platform.openai.com/docs/model-index-for-researchers a large language model tuned with reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) (Ouyang et al., 2022); Google’s Bard, based on PaLM (Chowdhery et al., 2022); Anthropic’s Claude,444https://www.anthropic.com/index/introducing-claude a 52B parameter model trained with reinforcement learning from AI feedback (Constitutional AI) Bai et al. (2022b), OpenAI’s GPT-4 (OpenAI, 2023), a large language model trained with RLHF, which is currently considered the state of the art. Responses from all baselines were sampled throughout April 2023.
items.zh: 我们将LIMA与五个基线进行比较：Alpaca 65B（Taori等人，2023）——我们在Alpaca训练集（Taori等人，2023）的52,000个示例上微调LLaMa 65B（Touvron等人，2023）；OpenAI的DaVinci003，一个通过基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）调整的大型语言模型（Ouyang等人，2022）；Google的Bard，基于PaLM（Chowdhery等人，2022）；Anthropic的Claude，一个通过基于AI反馈的强化学习（Constitutional AI）训练的52B参数模型（Bai等人，2022b）；OpenAI的GPT-4（OpenAI，2023），一个通过RLHF训练的大型语言模型，目前被认为是技术前沿。所有基线的响应均在2023年4月期间采样。

items.en: For each prompt, we generate a single response from each baseline model using nucleus sampling (Holtzman et al., 2019) with p=0.9𝑝0.9p=0.9 and a temperature of τ=0.7𝜏0.7\tau=0.7. We apply a repetition penalty of previously generated tokens with a hyperparameter of 1.2 (Keskar et al., 2019). We limit the maximum token length to 2048.
items.zh: 对于每个提示，我们使用核采样（Holtzman等人，2019）从每个基线模型生成一个响应，p=0.9，温度τ=0.7。我们对之前生成的标记应用重复惩罚，超参数为1.2（Keskar等人，2019）。我们将最大标记长度限制为2048。

items.en: At each step, we present annotators with a single prompt and two possible responses, generated by different models. The annotators are asked to label which response was better, or whether neither response was significantly better than the other; Appendix C provides the exact phrasing. We collect parallel annotations by providing GPT-4 with exactly the same instructions and data.
items.zh: 在每一步，我们向标注者展示一个提示和两个可能的响应，由不同模型生成。标注者被要求标记哪个响应更好，或者两个响应是否没有显著差异；附录C提供了确切的措辞。我们通过向GPT-4提供完全相同的指令和数据来收集并行标注。

items.en: We compute inter-annotator agreement using tie-discounted accuracy: we assign one point if both annotators agreed, half a point if either annotator (but not both) labeled a tie, and zero points otherwise. We measure agreement over a shared set of 50 annotation examples (single prompt, two model responses – all chosen randomly), comparing author, crowd, and GPT-4 annotations. Among human annotators, we find the following agreement scores: crowd-crowd 82%, crowd-author 81%, and author-author 78%. Despite some degree of subjectivity in this task, there is decent agreement among human annotators.
items.zh: 我们使用平局折扣准确率计算标注者间一致性：如果两个标注者一致，则分配1分；如果任一标注者（但不是两者）标记为平局，则分配0.5分；否则为0分。我们在共享的50个标注示例（单个提示，两个模型响应——全部随机选择）上测量一致性，比较作者、众包和GPT-4的标注。在人类标注者中，我们发现以下一致性分数：众包-众包82%，众包-作者81%，作者-作者78%。尽管此任务存在一定的主观性，但人类标注者之间的一致性相当好。

items.en: We also measure the agreement between GPT-4 and humans: crowd-GPT 78% and author-GPT 79% (although we use stochastic decoding, GPT-4 almost always agrees with itself). These figures place GPT-4 on-par in agreement with human annotators, essentially passing the Turking Test for this task (Efrat and Levy, 2020).
items.zh: 我们还测量了GPT-4与人类之间的一致性：众包-GPT 78%和作者-GPT 79%（尽管我们使用随机解码，GPT-4几乎总是与自己一致）。这些数字使GPT-4与人类标注者的一致性相当，基本上通过了此任务的Turking测试（Efrat和Levy，2020）。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Human preference evaluation, comparing LIMA to 5 different baselines across 300 test prompts.
items.capZh: 图1：人类偏好评估，将LIMA与5个不同基线在300个测试提示上进行比较。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Preference evaluation using GPT-4 as the annotator, given the same instructions provided to humans.
items.capZh: 图2：使用GPT-4作为标注者的偏好评估，提供与人类相同的指令。

items.en: While our main evaluation assesses LIMA with respect to state-of-the-art models, one must remember that some of these baselines are actually highly-tuned products that may have been exposed to millions of real user prompts during training, creating a very high bar. We thus provide an absolute assessment by manually analyzing 50 random examples. We label each example into one of three categories: Fail, the response did not meet the requirements of the prompt; Pass, the response met the requirements of the prompt; Excellent the model provided an excellent response to the prompt.
items.zh: 虽然我们的主要评估将LIMA与前沿模型进行比较，但必须记住，其中一些基线实际上是高度调整的产品，可能在训练过程中接触了数百万个真实用户提示，从而设置了非常高的标准。因此，我们通过手动分析50个随机示例提供绝对评估。我们将每个示例分为三类：失败，响应未满足提示要求；通过，响应满足提示要求；优秀，模型对提示提供了优秀的响应。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Analysis of LIMA over 50 test prompts.
items.capZh: 图3：LIMA在50个测试提示上的分析。

items.en: How does LIMA perform on examples Of the 50 analyzed examples, 43 have a training example that is somewhat related in terms of format (e.g. question answering, advice, letter writing, etc). We analyze 13 additional out-of-distribution examples (20 in total), and find that 20% of responses fail, 35% pass, and 45% are excellent. Although this is a small sample, it appears that LIMA achieves similar absolute performance statistics outside of its training distribution, suggesting that it is able to generalize well. Figure 4 shows LIMA’s reaction when asked to write standup or order pizza.
items.zh: LIMA在示例上的表现如何？在分析的50个示例中，有43个在格式上（例如问答、建议、写信等）有某种相关的训练示例。我们分析了13个额外的分布外示例（共20个），发现20%的响应失败，35%通过，45%优秀。尽管样本量较小，但LIMA在其训练分布之外似乎达到了类似的绝对性能统计，表明它能够很好地泛化。图4显示了LIMA在要求写单口喜剧或订披萨时的反应。

items.en: Finally, we analyze the effect of having a small number of safety-related examples in the training set (only 13; see Section 2.2). We check LIMA’s response to 30 potentially sensitive prompts from the test set, and find that LIMA responds safely to 80% of them (including 6 out of 10 prompts with malicious intent). In some cases, LIMA outright refuses to perform the task (e.g. when asked to provide a celebrity’s address), but when the malicious intent is implicit, LIMA is more likely to provide unsafe responses, as can be seen in Figure 4.
items.zh: 最后，我们分析了训练集中少量安全相关示例（仅13个；见第2.2节）的影响。我们检查LIMA对测试集中30个潜在敏感提示的响应，发现LIMA对其中80%的响应是安全的（包括10个恶意意图提示中的6个）。在某些情况下，LIMA直接拒绝执行任务（例如，当要求提供名人地址时），但当恶意意图隐含时，LIMA更可能提供不安全的响应，如图4所示。

full.sec: 5 Why is Less More? Ablations on Data Diversity, Quality, and Quantity
full.secZh: 5 为何少即是多？数据多样性、质量与数量的消融实验

items.en: We investigate the effects of training data diversity, quality, and quantity through ablation experiments. We observe that, for the purpose of alignment, scaling up input diversity and output quality have measurable positive effects, while scaling up quantity alone might not.
items.zh: 我们通过消融实验研究了训练数据多样性、质量和数量的影响。我们观察到，对于对齐而言，扩大输入多样性和输出质量具有可测量的积极影响，而仅扩大数量可能没有。

items.en: We fine-tune a 7B parameter LLaMa model Touvron et al. (2023) on various datasets, controlling for the same hyperparameters (Section 3).555While preliminary experiments show that it is possible to tune the 7B model with only 1,000 examples, we also found that using at least 2,000 examples improved stability in this setting. We then sample 5 responses for each test set prompt, and evaluate response quality by asking ChatGPT (GPT-3.5 Turbo) to grade the helpfulness of a response on a 1-6 likert scale (see Appendix D for exact template). We report the average score alongside a p=0.95𝑝0.95p=0.95 two-sided confidence interval.
items.zh: 我们在各种数据集上微调一个7B参数的LLaMa模型（Touvron等人，2023），控制相同的超参数（第3节）。虽然初步实验表明可以用仅1,000个示例调整7B模型，但我们也发现在此设置下使用至少2,000个示例可以提高稳定性。然后，我们为每个测试集提示采样5个响应，并通过要求ChatGPT（GPT-3.5 Turbo）在1-6 Likert量表上对响应的有用性进行评分来评估响应质量（确切模板见附录D）。我们报告平均分数以及p=0.95的双侧置信区间。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Performance of 7B models trained with 2,000 examples from different sources. Filtered Stack Exchange contains diverse prompts and high quality responses; Unfiltered Stack Exchange is diverse, but does not have any quality filters; wikiHow has high quality responses, but all of its prompts are “how to” questions.
items.capZh: 图5：使用来自不同来源的2,000个示例训练的7B模型的性能。过滤后的Stack Exchange包含多样化的提示和高质量的响应；未过滤的Stack Exchange多样化，但没有任何质量过滤器；wikiHow有高质量的响应，但其所有提示都是“如何做”问题。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Performance of 7B models trained with exponentially increasing amounts of data, sampled from (quality-filtered) Stack Exchange. Despite an up to 16-fold increase in data size, performance as measured by ChatGPT plateaus.
items.capZh: 图6：使用从Stack Exchange（质量过滤后）采样的指数增长数据量训练的7B模型的性能。尽管数据量增加了16倍，但ChatGPT测量的性能趋于平稳。

items.en: To test the effects of prompt diversity, while controlling for quality and quantity, we compare the effect of training on quality-filtered Stack Exchange data, which has heterogeneous prompts with excellent responses, and wikiHow data, which has homogeneous prompts with excellent responses. While we compare Stack Exchange with wikiHow as a proxy for diversity, we acknowledge that there may be other conflating factors when sampling data from two different sources. We sample 2,000 training examples from each source (following the same protocol from Section 2.1). Figure 6 shows that the more diverse Stack Exchange data yields significantly higher performance.
items.zh: 为了测试提示多样性的影响，同时控制质量和数量，我们比较了在质量过滤后的Stack Exchange数据（具有异质提示和优秀响应）和wikiHow数据（具有同质提示和优秀响应）上训练的效果。虽然我们将Stack Exchange与wikiHow作为多样性的代理进行比较，但我们承认从两个不同来源采样数据时可能存在其他混淆因素。我们从每个来源采样2,000个训练示例（遵循第2.1节的相同协议）。图6显示，更多样化的Stack Exchange数据产生了显著更高的性能。

items.en: To test the effects of response quality, we sample 2,000 examples from Stack Exchange without any quality or stylistic filters, and compare a model trained on this dataset to the one trained on our filtered dataset. Figure 6 shows that there is a significant 0.5 point difference between models trained on the filtered and unfiltered data sources.
items.zh: 为了测试响应质量的影响，我们从Stack Exchange中采样2,000个示例，没有任何质量或风格过滤器，并将在此数据集上训练的模型与在我们的过滤数据集上训练的模型进行比较。图6显示，在过滤和未过滤数据源上训练的模型之间存在显著的0.5分差异。

items.en: Scaling up the number of examples is a well-known strategy for improving performance in many machine learning settings. To test its effect on our setting, we sample exponentially increasing training sets from Stack Exchange. Figure 6 shows that, surprisingly, doubling the training set does not improve response quality. This result, alongside our other findings in this section, suggests that the scaling laws of alignment are not necessarily subject to quantity alone, but rather a function of prompt diversity while maintaining high quality responses.
items.zh: 扩大示例数量是许多机器学习设置中提高性能的众所周知策略。为了测试其在我们设置中的效果，我们从Stack Exchange中采样指数增长的训练集。图6显示，令人惊讶的是，将训练集加倍并没有提高响应质量。这一结果，连同我们在本节中的其他发现，表明对齐的缩放定律不一定只受数量影响，而是提示多样性和保持高质量响应的函数。

items.en: Can a model fine-tuned on only 1,000 single-turn interactions engage in multi-turn dialogue? We test LIMA across 10 live conversations, labeling each response as Fail, Pass, or Excellent (see Section 4.3). LIMA responses are surprisingly coherent for a zero-shot chatbot, referencing information from previous steps in the dialogue. It is clear though that the model is operating out of distribution; in 6 out of 10 conversations, LIMA fails to follow the prompt within 3 interactions.
items.zh: 一个仅在1,000个单轮交互上微调的模型能否进行多轮对话？我们在10个实时对话中测试LIMA，将每个响应标记为失败、通过或优秀（见第4.3节）。LIMA的响应对于零样本聊天机器人来说出奇地连贯，引用了对话中先前步骤的信息。但很明显，模型在分布外运行；在10个对话中的6个中，LIMA在3次交互内未能遵循提示。

items.capEn: Figure 7: Analysis of dialogue turns, averaged over 10 test chats.
items.capZh: 图7：对话轮次分析，平均于10个测试聊天。

items.en: To improve its ability to converse, we gather 30 multi-turn dialogue chains. Among these, 10 dialogues are composed by the authors, while the remaining 20 are based on comment chains from Stack Exchange, which we edit to fit the assistant’s style. We fine-tune a new version of LIMA from the pretrained LLaMa model using the combined 1,030 examples, and conduct 10 live conversations based on the same prompts used for the zero-shot model. Figure 8 shows excerpts from such dialogues.
items.zh: 为了提高对话能力，我们收集了30个多轮对话链。其中，10个对话由作者编写，其余20个基于Stack Exchange的评论链，我们对其进行了编辑以符合助手的风格。我们使用合并后的1,030个示例，从预训练的LLaMa模型微调出一个新版本的LIMA，并基于与零样本模型相同的提示进行了10次实时对话。图8展示了这些对话的摘录。

items.en: We show that fine-tuning a strong pretrained language model on 1,000 carefully curated examples can produce remarkable, competitive results on a wide range of prompts. However, there are limitations to this approach. Primarily, the mental effort in constructing such examples is significant and difficult to scale up. Secondly, LIMA is not as robust as product-grade models; while LIMA typically generates good responses, an unlucky sample during decoding or an adversarial prompt can often lead to a weak response. That said, the evidence presented in this work demonstrates the potential of tackling the complex issues of alignment with a simple approach.
items.zh: 我们证明，在1,000个精心策划的示例上微调一个强大的预训练语言模型，可以在广泛的提示上产生显著且具有竞争力的结果。然而，这种方法存在局限性。首先，构建此类示例的心智负担很大且难以扩展。其次，LIMA不如产品级模型稳健；虽然LIMA通常能生成良好的响应，但解码过程中的不幸采样或对抗性提示往往会导致较弱的响应。尽管如此，本工作提供的证据表明，用简单方法解决复杂的对齐问题具有潜力。
